ops.title: cc-sudo is a bootstrapped skeleton; the actual Touch ID sudo flow is the next piece of work
ops.body: cc-sudo was bootstrapped on 2026-07-14 (repo-bootstrap swift layer, features `release`, extras `plugin,superset`) and released as v0.1.0 — a signed/notarized universal binary cask on `yasyf/tap`. Only the `hello` smoke command exists. The product — Claude runs a sudo command, it surfaces to the user as a native macOS Touch ID prompt (LocalAuthentication `LAContext`), approval runs it and returns stdout/stderr/exit code, denial returns a clean non-zero exit — is designed in the README use cases but not built. The `plugin/scripts/install-binary.sh` installer was scaffolded (extra `plugin`) but no plugin manifest exists yet; wiring it into Claude Code sessions is part of the product work. Logic goes in `Sources/CCSudo` (library), the executable target `Sources/cc-sudo` stays a thin ArgumentParser shell.
